parsed_review.summary: CP00-730 (RP24 Korean Legal Depth P02 Hermes/Claude bridge, Risk B, 40 units RP24.P02.M07.S13-RP24.P02.M09.S08) passes independent read-only review. I recounted all 40 units from review-baseline.json: deliverable distribution (implementation 16, claude_review 2, ui 4, failure_recovery 4, test 8, contract 2, security_audit 4) and micro-title distribution (Test And Golden Case Set 10, Hermes Evidence Packet 22, Claude Review Packet 8) match the pack baseline exactly. Implementation is descriptor-only and runtime-closed: registry.js CP00-730 binding is correct (next_pack_id CP00-731, next_subphase_id RP24.P02.M09.S09, flag korean_legal_p02_hermes_claude_bridge_descriptor_verified); service.js createDescriptor/createHermesEvidencePacket/createClaudeReviewPacket set emits_runtime_receipt=false, writes_product_state=false, read_only=true; validators.js enforces descriptor_only, no sensitive-payload leakage, no HWPX/court-filing/litigation/corporate/clause runtime opening, deny-over-allow permission baseline, no audit event bodies, and the authority boundary (claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false). model.test.js covers CP00-730 (binding/coverage, runtime-closed descriptor/packets, contract projection latest with CP722-729 historical), and the package.json test script includes packages/korean-legal/test. No-write/no-real-data boundaries hold: the only filesystem write is the deterministic Korean Legal Depth contract projection (a repo build artifact regenerated by the validate script), not product/database/object-storage state. Hermes patterns are used only as deterministic gate/packet descriptors, consistent with hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false. The pack correctly does NOT yet assert production_ready: manifest (production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, descriptor_only=true), construction-inspection (verdict PENDING_REVIEW), command-evidence (status in_progress), and adjudication.md all sit pending exactly one valid hardened read-only Claude review receipt — this receipt. CP178+ hardening checks pass: readiness auth preflight succeeded (exit 0, valid result JSON), and claude-review-result.json presents no invalid attempts as valid (valid_review_receipts=[], exactly_one_valid_pack_level_claude_review_run=false, the expected pre-review state). The 10-command validation ladder plus syntax/build checks are recorded exit 0 in command-evidence.json; this is agent-reported and not re-run under read-only tooling, but every referenced npm script and validator exists. Claude remains a read-only, non-final reviewer. No P0/P1/P2 findings; one P3 informational note documenting the contract-artifact write boundary. Verdict PASS; does not block pack closeout or this CP closeout goal. Final human/enterprise-trust approval remains outside this receipt by design.

findings.title: Sole filesystem write is the deterministic Korean Legal Depth contract projection, not product state
findings.details: The pack-wide no-write attestation (KOREAN_LEGAL_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION: writes_product_state=false, creates_database_rows=false, updates_database_rows=false, writes_object_storage=false) is honored. The only writeFile in the active implementation scope is the regeneration of contracts/korean-legal-depth-contract.json, which is a deterministic, idempotent repo contract/projection artifact built from frozen descriptors — not product runtime state, a database row, or object storage. The descriptors are deep-frozen and the projection asserts validation.valid=true with latest_pack=CP00-730 and CP722-729 historical, matching the model.test.js contract assertions. This is by design and consistent with prior packs CP722-729; it does not contradict the no-product-state-write boundary.
findings.recommendation: No action required. Continue treating the contract projection as a generated build artifact (regenerated by npm run rp24:korean-legal-depth:validate); do not reclassify it as product state.
